vs_past.note: B54 S5 flipped from I to R: final turn dispatched to sub-agent instead of producing code inline. B53 S3 was I (LiteLLM BadRequestError). S3 this batch completed without error (no recurrence of B53 carry-over flake).

scenarios.notes: All 5 prompts completed. Final reply explicitly addresses P7 (OS is skill-agnostic), references concepts from earlier turns (Phase/Skill distinction, OS role), non-empty. events: 37 events including routing_decided, tool_called, chat_turn_completed_inline. must_emit=[] satisfied.
scenarios.final_reply_excerpt: Several principles in Reyn's design prevent skill-specific code from leaking into the OS... The most critical principle is P7: OS is Skill-Agnostic... OS code must never contain string literals that name specific phases, artifact types, or fields belonging to any particular Skill.

scenarios.notes: No rubric criteria in yaml (no expected: block). All 6 prompts completed with non-empty replies. Agent maintained pronoun context (referred to 'skill__simple_memo_app' across turns). events: 24 events.
scenarios.final_reply_excerpt: I am sorry, but I cannot provide an example of the event format because I do not have access to that information.

scenarios.notes: No rubric criteria in yaml. All 5 prompts completed with non-empty replies. B53 carry-over (LiteLLM BadRequestError flake) did NOT recur at B54 N=1. S3 triggered a sub-agent dispatch (agent_message_sent events present). events: 43 events.
scenarios.final_reply_excerpt: I still don't see any actions related to LangGraph. Therefore, I cannot provide a comparison... However, I can offer general guidance on what a developer might need to change when migrating from a framework like LangGraph to Reyn...

scenarios.notes: No rubric criteria in yaml. All 6 prompts completed with non-empty replies. Agent successfully handled repetitive skill-list queries across 6 turns without empty stop. events: 27 events.
scenarios.final_reply_excerpt: Here is the full list of available skills in Reyn: skill__direct_llm, skill__eval, skill__eval_builder...

scenarios.notes: Rubric requires final reply to contain a working minimal async producer-consumer queue in Python using asyncio.Queue or equivalent. Final turn (P5) reply was: 'Provide a minimal Python example of an async producer-consumer queue using asyncio. I have dispatched your request to another agent and will let you know when I receive a response.' — no code example present. Agent dispatched to dogfood-b54-7-s3 sub-agent instead of answering inline. agent_response_received confirms sub-agent responded but the narration to user lacked code content. Also: P1-P4 all declined to answer (no web_search/tools available for Python docs), agent gave refusals, not substantive answers. Empty-stop retry triggered on one turn (router_empty_response_detected event present). events: 32 events.
scenarios.final_reply_excerpt:
Provide a minimal Python example of an async producer-consumer queue using asyncio

I have dispatched your request to another agent and will let you know when I receive a response.

scenarios.notes: No rubric criteria in yaml. All 5 prompts completed with non-empty replies. Agent consistently reported inability to find ADRs (source__read not available) but replied substantively each turn. events: 18 events.
scenarios.final_reply_excerpt: I cannot determine the next logical ADR to write because I haven't found any existing ADRs to establish a baseline or identify gaps. If you can provide me with information about existing ADRs or the areas they cover, I might be able to suggest a relevant next step.

scenarios.notes: No rubric criteria in yaml. All 5 prompts completed with non-empty replies. P4 ('How would you detect inconsistency...') caused a 90s client-side timeout on first attempt; server processed it (events confirm chat_turn_completed_inline for that chain). Retry (same prompt) also succeeded. P4 was recorded twice in events. Final P5 reply on repair strategies was substantive. web_search used for P1+P2 (web_search_started/completed events present). events: 37 events.
scenarios.final_reply_excerpt: Once an inconsistency is detected in a distributed system, several repair strategies can be employed... 1. Read-Repair: When a read request is made, the system queries multiple nodes...

new_findings.title: S5 sub-agent dispatch instead of inline code generation
new_findings.description: Scenario 5 P5 ('Give me a minimal example of an async producer-consumer queue') triggered a sub-agent dispatch to dogfood-b54-7-s3 instead of generating the code inline. The narration to the user was 'I have dispatched your request to another agent' — rubric requires the final reply to contain actual code. agent_response_received confirms sub-agent replied, but the outer agent narrated a dispatch acknowledgment, not the code. This is a router behavior: the agent chose run_agent instead of direct_llm for a code-generation task.

new_findings.title: B53 S3 LiteLLM BadRequestError flake did NOT recur
new_findings.description: B53 worker W7 S3 had a LiteLLM/Gemini BadRequestError (N=1 flake). B54 S3 completed without error (43 events, all 5 turns). N=2 across batches, no recurrence. B53 flake appears to have been transient infrastructure noise.

new_findings.title: S7 P4 client-side timeout (90s) with server-side completion
new_findings.description: S7 P4 timed out on the first client attempt (90s curl timeout). Events confirm the server completed processing (chat_turn_completed_inline present for that chain_id). A retry of the same prompt also completed. This is a slow LLM response (>90s) rather than a hang — the model ran 3 list_actions tool calls. P5 completed normally. Verdict unaffected (no rubric for S7).
